qualifying_org_definition: Any public or nonprofit organization where the ABAWD performs volunteer/community service work. No pre-approved list or mandatory site registration required — clients may identify their own qualifying site. DTA offers an optional Volunteer Site Form for nonprofits wishing to be listed on SNAPPathtoWork.org, but completion is not a prerequisite for hours to count.
verification_instrument: Community Service Participation Report (also referred to as ABAWD Work Program Participation Report, WPPR-EN Rev. 4/2025, form 09-323-0917-05) — the volunteer coordinator (nonprofit staff) fills out and signs the site section; recipient submits to DTA at each reporting period. This is the 'form approved by the Department' referenced in 106 CMR 362.320; it is completed after the fact and does not require prior DTA site approval.
hours_formula_detail: SNAP benefit allotment ÷ Massachusetts state minimum wage ($15/hr as of 2026). The WPPR form states explicitly: 'The number of hours that you must volunteer is determined by dividing your monthly SNAP benefit by the current Massachusetts minimum wage.' Example given: $150 benefit ÷ $15 = 10 hours/month. This is NOT the federal 80-hour floor — MA uses the state minimum wage, not the federal $7.25 rate. A max-allotment single adult (~$291/mo in FY2026) would yield ~19-20 hours/month.
accepts_outofstate_detail: The WPPR form (Rev. 4/2025) and all DTA guidance refer only to 'a non-profit or public organization' with no geographic restriction on location. The Volunteer Site Guide and WPPR language do not require the organization to be Massachusetts-based or registered in MA. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit is acceptable absent a state restriction. MA policy is silent on this point, so out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted, but no primary source explicitly confirms it.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Massachusetts held a statewide ABAWD waiver for many years but it expired/was terminated by 2026. As of the OBBBA era, no active waivers are listed for MA. USDA listed no active MA waivers as of early 2026.
submission_deadline: unknown — the WPPR form does not state a specific deadline (e.g., 'by 10th of following month'). Recipients submit the form to DTA after completing volunteer hours; the form can cover a prior month retroactively. No explicit cutoff date found in primary sources.
notes:
Massachusetts is Tier 1 clean: the general volunteer/community service lane requires no EDP, county assignment, MOU, or mandatory site pre-registration. Clients may volunteer at any public or nonprofit organization and verify hours via the WPPR form signed by the volunteer coordinator. The DTA Volunteer Site Form and SNAPPathtoWork.org listing are optional for nonprofits and are not a gate on whether hours count. Confidence is medium rather than high because the primary Mass.gov and mass.gov/doc PDF sources returned HTTP 403 and could not be directly read — findings rely on the SomeDoing.com synthesis, MLRI 2025 guide citation snippets from search results, and the search result excerpts from official DTA materials; the Volunteer Site Form's optional vs. required status could not be confirmed from the primary DTA document text.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial searches and direct primary-source review confirm Tier 1 clean with high confidence. The official DTA ABAWD Consolidated Notice (form 4148-04, retrieved from eohhs.ehs.state.ma.us/DTA/PolicyOnline/olg docs/Consolidated Notice Sample.pdf) was successfully read in full. It states explicitly under 'How do I meet the ABAWD Work Rules by volunteering?': 'You can also find your own non-profit or public volunteer site in your community, such as at a public library or a food pantry.' Verification is described as completing a 'Community Service Participation Report' — a post-hoc form, not a prior approval gate. There is no EDP, case manager meeting, or enrollment step described anywhere in the notice before volunteer hours count. The notice goes directly from ABAWD obligation to the two options (work 80 hrs/month OR volunteer at any nonprofit/public org). 106 CMR 362.320 (read via Cornell LII) uses the phrase 'approved by the Department' for volunteer placements, but in context this refers to the department-approved reporting form (the WPPR/Community Service Participation Report), not prior site registration — confirmed by the notice's self-arrange language. General SNAP work registration under 106 CMR 362.310 is a separate baseline requirement (register at application, every 12 months), not a gating prestep specific to the ABAWD volunteer lane. Adversarial searches for 'mandatory work registration intake before volunteer hours,' 'pre-approved site list,' and 'SNAP E&T enrollment required before volunteering' all returned no evidence of such requirements in MA. The SNAP Path to Work (E&T) program is described as a separate optional/voluntary track and is not a prerequisite for the direct community service volunteer lane. Remote/online volunteering remains unaddressed in all sources reviewed — the regulation and notice are silent on remote work, so remote_stance remains 'unknown'. Confidence raised to 'high': Tier 1 clean is confirmed by the official DTA client-facing notice (a primary DTA document), 106 CMR 362.320 regulatory text, and consistent with 2025-2026 MLRI guidance. All material fields (general_volunteer_lane, required_prestep, hours_reporting) are now resolved from primary sources.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): WPPR form (Rev. 4/2025, form 09-323-0917-05) was successfully extracted via pypdf from the DTA policy site. Key findings: (1) HOURS FORMULA — MA uses state-minimum-wage workfare formula, NOT the federal 80-hour standard. The form states verbatim: 'The number of hours that you must volunteer is determined by dividing your monthly SNAP benefit by the current Massachusetts minimum wage.' At MA's $15/hr minimum wage, a max-allotment single adult (~$291/mo) yields ~19-20 hours/month. monthly_hours_required set to 20 as a representative figure. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT — Form and all DTA guidance say 'non-profit or public organization' with no geographic restriction. Policy is silent; federal default permits any qualifying nonprofit. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS — MA's longstanding statewide waiver expired/terminated; no active waivers as of 2026. Fully enforced statewide under OBBBA rules. (4) SUBMISSION MECHANISM — WPPR form lists: DTA Connect online portal, DTA Connect mobile app, mail to P.O. Box 4406 Taunton MA, fax to 617-887-8765, or scan at local DTA office (paper_county_office). (5) FORM FIELDS — Part 2 of the WPPR requires: recipient name, APID (case number), recipient address, org name, org address, org phone, hours total per month, start date, printed name + title + signature of staff person. No EIN, no notarization, no DOB required.
